name: Managed OIDC Integration
description: Delegate authentication to managed providers via OpenID Connect
when_to_use: Apps needing secure social login enterprise SSO and reduced security liability
trade_offs: Adds dependency on third-party uptime and per-user cost
implementation_notes: Use PKCE flow for clients exchange codes for JWTs and verify signatures locally
keywords: oidc oauth2 auth0 clerk okta sso identity

name: Idempotent Payment Processor
description: Safe transaction handling with external payment gateways
when_to_use: E-commerce subscriptions and any financial transaction where retries occur
trade_offs: Requires storing idempotency keys and handling partial states
implementation_notes: Pass unique Idempotency-Key in headers and return cached result on duplicates

name: Webhook HMAC Verification
description: Verify authenticity of incoming events from external providers
when_to_use: Receiving payment confirmations user lifecycle events and third-party triggers
trade_offs: Slight CPU overhead for hashing and requires secret management
implementation_notes: Compute HMAC-SHA256 of raw body using shared secret and compare with header

name: Async Notification Outbox
description: Offload external communication to background workers
when_to_use: Sending emails SMS or push notifications via Twilio/SendGrid/OneSignal
trade_offs: Increases latency between action and delivery but improves reliability
implementation_notes: Save notification intent in DB in same transaction as action then poll/stream to worker
keywords: outbox pattern twilio sendgrid email sms worker

name: Streaming LLM Gateway
description: Proxy and manage calls to LLM providers like OpenAI or Anthropic
when_to_use: AI-powered apps needing real-time streaming responses and cost control
trade_offs: Requires handling Server-Sent Events (SSE) and partial JSON
implementation_notes: Implement stream proxy with budget limits rate limiting and prompt logging
keywords: openai anthropic llm streaming sse gateway

name: Agentic Tool Gateway (MCP)
description: Implement Model Context Protocol for AI Agent tools
when_to_use: Exposing backend capabilities to AI agents as executable tools
trade_offs: Requires strict schema definition and security sandboxing
implementation_notes: Expose JSON-RPC endpoints with clear tool descriptions and safety checks
keywords: mcp model context protocol agent tools ai

name: Secure Object Proxy
description: Manage file access via signed URLs and object storage
when_to_use: User uploads media sharing and private document management
trade_offs: Requires short-lived secrets and metadata synchronization
implementation_notes: Issue short-lived signed GET/PUT URLs and use webhooks for status sync
keywords: s3 presigned url signed url cloud storage

name: Search Engine Sync
description: Sync database records with external search engines like Algolia
when_to_use: Apps needing fast full-text search faceted filtering and typo tolerance
implementation_notes: Use Change Data Capture (CDC) or hooks to push updates to search index

name: External Status Aggregator
description: Integrate status page data from critical dependencies
when_to_use: Dashboarding and automated failover for multi-cloud/SaaS apps
trade_offs: Adds complexity to health check logic
implementation_notes: Poll dependency status APIs and expose aggregate health via /health/deps

name: Geocoding and Map Proxy
description: Integrate Google Maps or Mapbox with caching and privacy
when_to_use: Store locators delivery tracking and address validation
trade_offs: Map APIs are expensive and have strict caching TOS
implementation_notes: Proxy map requests to hide client IP and cache non-PII geocoding results
keywords: google maps mapbox geocoding location proxy

name: Headless Task Integration
description: Trigger and monitor long-running automation tasks
when_to_use: Web scraping PDF generation and scheduled reporting via Apify/Browserless
trade_offs: Requires handling long-lived job states and timeouts
implementation_notes: Use async job pattern with status polling or webhooks for completion

name: External Key Management (KMS)
description: Delegate encryption/decryption to managed KMS providers
when_to_use: Encrypting sensitive PII fields and managing database encryption keys
trade_offs: Increases latency for every encrypted write/read
implementation_notes: Use envelope encryption: fetch data key from KMS then encrypt locally
keywords: kms aws kms google kms vault encryption

name: External Authorization (FGA)
description: Offload complex permission checks to Fine-Grained Auth providers
when_to_use: Multi-tenant SaaS with complex RBAC/ABAC and nested permissions
trade_offs: Latency of external auth check can impact UI/API responsiveness
implementation_notes: Use Zanzibar-inspired providers (AuthZed/Okta FGA) and cache decisions

name: Edge Config Integration
description: Fetch feature flags and config from edge providers
when_to_use: Global apps needing ultra-low latency configuration changes
trade_offs: Updates may have slight propagation delay (seconds)
implementation_notes: Fetch from Edge Config (Vercel/Cloudflare) and fallback to local cache
keywords: vercel edge config cloudflare workers feature flag

name: Managed Message Broker
description: Integrate with serverless pub/sub or queues
when_to_use: Decoupled services using Ably/Pusher or AWS EventBridge
trade_offs: Requires vendor-specific client libraries and retry logic
implementation_notes: Abstract broker behind an interface and use dead-letter queues for failures
keywords: ably pusher eventbridge pubsub event driven

name: Vector DB Sync
description: Keeping vector embeddings in sync with primary data
when_to_use: RAG-based AI applications and semantic search
trade_offs: Embedding generation is expensive and async by nature
implementation_notes: Trigger embedding generation in background when text fields change in DB
keywords: pinecone weaviate vector search embeddings rag
